discoveries.title: Aetna: Tumor Markers (CPB 0352) Updated
discoveries.summary: This Aetna policy document primarily denies coverage for ctDNA, liquid biopsy, and MRD testing approaches, with the content focusing on traditional tumor markers rather than circulating tumor DNA technologies. The policy lists multiple named tests (signatera, guardant-reveal, foundationone-liquid, etc.) and MRD billing codes but appears to classify them as not medically necessary.

analysis.summary: This Aetna policy document primarily denies coverage for ctDNA, liquid biopsy, and MRD testing approaches, with the content focusing on traditional tumor markers rather than circulating tumor DNA technologies. The policy lists multiple named tests (signatera, guardant-reveal, foundationone-liquid, etc.) and MRD billing codes but appears to classify them as not medically necessary.

Excludes germline mutations (refers to separate policy CPB 0140)

discoveries.title: UnitedHealthcare: Molecular Oncology Testing for Cancer (Solid Tumors) Updated
discoveries.summary: UnitedHealthcare denies coverage for most ctDNA and liquid biopsy tests, explicitly listing MRD assays (including Signatera, Guardant Reveal, RaDaR, Haystack MRD) and multi-cancer early detection tests (like Galleri) as unproven and not medically necessary. Limited coverage is provided only for liquid biopsy multigene profiling (≤50 genes) specifically for non-small cell lung cancer.

Limited coverage is provided only for liquid biopsy multigene profiling (≤50 genes) specifically for non-small cell lung cancer.

discoveries.title: UnitedHealthcare: Molecular Oncology Testing for Hematologic Cancer Diagnosis Updated
discoveries.summary: This policy focuses on hematologic cancer testing and explicitly denies coverage for molecular tests other than clonoSEQ for MRD assessment. While it covers clonoSEQ for MRD testing in specific blood cancers, it restricts other molecular oncology tests and references a separate policy for solid tumor testing.

discoveries.title: EviCore (Evernorth): Multi-Cancer Early Detection Screening (MOL.TS.396.A) Updated
discoveries.summary: EviCore denies coverage for multi-cancer early detection (MCED) screening tests, classifying them as experimental, investigational, or unproven due to insufficient evidence of clinical utility. The policy specifically states there is no evidence supporting ctDNA assays for cancer screening outside of clinical trials.

analysis.summary: EviCore denies coverage for multi-cancer early detection (MCED) screening tests, classifying them as experimental, investigational, or unproven due to insufficient evidence of clinical utility. The policy specifically states there is no evidence supporting ctDNA assays for cancer screening outside of clinical trials.

discoveries.title: Carelon (formerly AIM Specialty Health): Genetic Liquid Biopsy in Cancer Management - Landing Page Updated
discoveries.summary: Carelon has established clinical appropriateness guidelines for genetic liquid biopsy testing in cancer management and surveillance, effective November 15, 2025, with updated codes as of January 1, 2026. The policy appears to take a restrictive stance, requiring specific clinical criteria to be met and stating that genetic tests not specifically mentioned in the guidelines are considered not medically necessary.

metadata.policyName: Genetic Liquid Biopsy in Cancer Management - Landing Page

analysis.summary: Carelon has established clinical appropriateness guidelines for genetic liquid biopsy testing in cancer management and surveillance, effective November 15, 2025, with updated codes as of January 1, 2026. The policy appears to take a restrictive stance, requiring specific clinical criteria to be met and stating that genetic tests not specifically mentioned in the guidelines are considered not medically necessary.
keyConditions: Genomic testing must have established analytical and clinical validity
Must be performed in an appropriately certified laboratory
Testing limited to evaluation following an intervention or when there is a change in clinical status
Must demonstrate reasonable likelihood that intervention will change management and/or lead to improved patient outcomes
Genetic tests not specifically mentioned in guidelines are considered not medically necessary
May require peer-to-peer conversation for appropriateness determination

discoveries.title: BCBS Michigan: Circulating Tumor DNA Updated
discoveries.summary: BCBS Michigan covers ctDNA/liquid biopsy testing only for selecting targeted therapy in advanced solid cancers when specific criteria are met, but denies coverage for MRD testing and cancer screening applications including the Galleri test. Coverage requires advanced/metastatic cancer, inability to perform tissue testing or FDA-approved companion diagnostic status, and no prior testing with the same panel.

discoveries.title: Highmark BCBS: NavDx HPV ctDNA Testing (L-267) Updated
discoveries.summary: Highmark BCBS covers NavDx HPV ctDNA testing for patients with HPV-driven head and neck cancer when performed either prior to surgery or within 5 years post-operatively. The policy establishes specific medical necessity criteria for this liquid biopsy test rather than denying coverage outright.

discoveries.title: Blue Cross NC: Liquid Biopsy (AHS-G2054) Updated
discoveries.summary: Blue Cross NC provides coverage for liquid biopsy/ctDNA testing in specific clinical scenarios for NSCLC (when tissue testing is inadequate), HR-positive/HER2-negative breast cancer (PIK3CA testing), and castration-resistant prostate cancer (AR-V7, BRCA1/2). However, screening tests like Galleri and most other liquid biopsy applications are considered investigational and not covered.

analysis.summary: Blue Cross NC provides coverage for liquid biopsy/ctDNA testing in specific clinical scenarios for NSCLC (when tissue testing is inadequate), HR-positive/HER2-negative breast cancer (PIK3CA testing), and castration-resistant prostate cancer (AR-V7, BRCA1/2). However, screening tests like Galleri and most other liquid biopsy applications are considered investigational and not covered.

Must be for treatment selection, not screening or general monitoring

discoveries.title: BlueCross BlueShield South Carolina: Liquid Biopsy Updated
discoveries.summary: BlueCross BlueShield South Carolina provides limited coverage for ctDNA/liquid biopsy testing in specific clinical scenarios for NSCLC, advanced breast cancer, and castration-resistant prostate cancer, but denies coverage for screening applications and most other uses. The policy explicitly states that liquid biopsy testing for screening (including GRAIL Galleri) and most other malignancy detection/monitoring is considered not medically necessary.

analysis.summary: BlueCross BlueShield South Carolina provides limited coverage for ctDNA/liquid biopsy testing in specific clinical scenarios for NSCLC, advanced breast cancer, and castration-resistant prostate cancer, but denies coverage for screening applications and most other uses. keyConditions: NSCLC: Coverage when tissue-based testing is infeasible, insufficient tissue available, incomplete biomarker assessment, or concurrent with tissue testing
Advanced breast cancer (recurrent/unresectable or stage IV): Coverage for specific mutations (AKT1, ESR1, PIK3CA, NTRK, RET, PTEN) when concurrent with tissue testing or tissue testing was negative
Castration-resistant prostate cancer: Coverage for AR-V7 and BRCA1/BRCA2 testing for therapy selection
Intermediate-risk microhematuria: Coverage for Cxbladder Triage with specific patient acknowledgment conditions
Annual testing allowed for qualifying individuals
